overview:
## Discussion

When you create a contextual embedding, the model isn’t loaded until you need it, by default. Use [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/load()] and [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/unload()] to control when to load and unload the model.

The method fails if the necessary assets — for the model you specify — aren’t on device. Use [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/hasAvailableAssets] and [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/requestAssets(completionHandler:)] to manage the assets.

# load()

**Instance Method**

Loads the embedding model.

## Declaration

```swift
func load() throws
```

## Discussion

When you create a contextual embedding, the model isn’t loaded until you need it, by default. Use [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/load()] and [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/unload()] to control when to load and unload the model.

The method fails if the necessary assets — for the model you specify — aren’t on device. Use [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/hasAvailableAssets] and [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/requestAssets(completionHandler:)] to manage the assets.

## Loading and unloading assets

- **unload()**: Unloads the embedding model.
title: load()
